maml:description.maml:para: Converts .NET type names to LLM (Language Model) type names.
Takes a .NET type name as input and returns the corresponding simplified type name used in Language Models. It handles common .NET types and provides appropriate type mappings.
The .NET type name to convert to an LLM type name
The .NET type name to convert to an LLM type name
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Converts a PSCustomObject to a HashTable recursively.
* This function converts a PSCustomObject and all its nested PSCustomObject properties into HashTables. * It handles arrays and other collection types by processing each element recursively.
The PSCustomObject to convert into a HashTable
The PSCustomObject to convert into a HashTable
Accepts System.Object objects via pipeline.
Returns objects.
Returns objects.
Returns objects.
Returns objects.

command:example.maml:title: C

maml:description.maml:para: Parses strings for any valid URI.
* Extracts all valid URIs from input text, supporting standard and custom URI schemes like http:, https:, ftp:, magnet:, about:, etc. * Returns Uri objects for each valid URI found.
Text input that may contain URIs
Text input that may contain URIs
Accepts System.String[] objects via pipeline.
Copies parameter values from bound parameters to a new hashtable based on another function's possible parameters.

* This cmdlet creates a new hashtable containing only the parameter values that match the parameters defined in the specified target function. * The resulting hashtable can then be used to invoke the function using PowerShell splatting. * Switch parameters are only included in the result if they were explicitly provided and set to $true in the bound parameters. * Non-present switch parameters are excluded from the result to maintain proper parameter semantics.
Source bound parameters to copy from
Target function name to filter parameters
Default values for parameters
Source bound parameters to copy from
Target function name to filter parameters
Default values for parameters
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Search for files and directories with advanced filtering options.

* Find files with Find-Item -> l * Fast multi-threaded search: utilizes parallel and asynchronous IO processing with configurable maximum degree of parallelism (default based on CPU cores) for efficient file and directory scanning. * Advanced Pattern Matching: Supports wildcards (*, ?), recursive patterns like **, and complex path structures for precise file and directory queries. **/filename will only recurse until filename is matched. multiple of these patterns are allowed, as long as the are preceeded with a filename or directoryname to match. This pattern parser has the power of Resolve-Path but has recursion features, and does only support * and ? as wildcards, preventing bugs with paths with [ ] brackets in them, eliminating the need for -LiteralPath parameter, while maintaining integrity for paths sections without wildcards, unlike a wildcard match on the whole full path. * Enhanced Content Searching: Comprehensive Select-String integration with regular expression patterns within file contents using the -Content parameter. * Large File Optimization: Handles extremely large files with smart overlapping buffers and minimal heap allocation * Multiple Match Options: Find all matches per line (-AllMatches) or just the first match per file(-List) * Case Sensitivity Control: Case-sensitive matching(-CaseSensitive) with culture-specific options(-Culture) * Context Capture: Show lines before and after matches(-Context) for better understanding *Inverse Matching: Find files that don't contain the pattern (-NotMatch) * Output Formats: Raw string output(-Raw), quiet boolean response(-Quiet), or full MatchInfo objects * Pattern Types: Regular expressions(default) or simple literal string matching(-SimpleMatch) * Encoding Support: Specify file encoding(-Encoding) for accurate text processing * Path Type Flexibility: Handles relative, absolute, UNC, rooted paths, and NTFS alternate data streams(ADS) with optional content search in streams. * Multi-Drive Support: Searches across all drives with - AllDrives or specific drives via -SearchDrives, including optical disks if specified. * Directory and File Filtering: Options to search directories only(-Directory), both files and directories(-FilesAndDirectories), or files with content matching. * Exclusion and Limits: Exclude patterns with -Exclude, set max recursion depth -MaxRecursionDepth), file size limits(-MaxFileSize, -MinFileSize), and modified date filters(-ModifiedAfter, -ModifiedBefore). * Output Customization: Supports PassThru for FileInfo/DirectoryInfo objects, relative paths, hyperlinks in attended mode, or plain paths in unattended mode (use -NoLinks in case of mishaps to enforce unattended mode). * Performance Optimizations: Skips non-text files by default for content search (override with -IncludeNonTextFileMatching), handles long paths(>260 chars), and follows symlinks/junctions. * Safety Features: Timeout support(-TimeoutSeconds), ignores inaccessible items, skips system attributes by default, and prevents infinite loops with visited node tracking.

* Valid categories for the -Category parameter are: Pictures, Videos, Music, Documents, Spreadsheets, Presentations, Archives, Installers, Executables, Databases, DesignFiles, Ebooks, Subtitles, Fonts, EmailFiles, 3DModels, GameAssets, MedicalFiles, FinancialFiles, LegalFiles, SourceCode, Scripts, MarkupAndData, Configuration, Logs, TextFiles, WebFiles, MusicLyricsAndChords, CreativeWriting, Recipes, ResearchFiles

command:parameter.aliases: like, Path, LiteralPath, Query, SearchMask, Include
command:parameter.maml:name: Name

maml:description.maml:para: File name or pattern to search for. Default is '*'
Regular expression pattern to search within content
Base path for resolving relative paths in output
Search across all available drives
Indicates that the cmdlet searches for more than one match in each line of text. Without this parameter, Select-String finds only the first match in each line of text.
File attributes to skip (e.g., System, Hidden or None).
Gets or sets the case-sensitivity for files and directories
Indicates that the cmdlet matches are case-sensitive. By default, matches aren't case-sensitive.
The Category parameter.
Captures the specified number of lines before and after the line that matches the pattern.

Specifies a culture name to match the specified pattern. The Culture parameter must be used with the SimpleMatch parameter. The default behavior uses the culture of the current PowerShell runspace (session).
Search for directories only
Optional: search specific drives
Specifies the type of encoding for the target file. Supports Select-String compatible values and extended .NET encodings.
Exclude files or directories matching these wildcard patterns (e.g., *.tmp, *\bin\*).
Include both files and directories
Follow symlinks and junctions during directory traversal
Include alternate data streams in search results
Include non-text files when searching file contents
Include optical disk drives
File name or pattern to search for. Default is '*'
Enforces searching only within Root directories by stripping directory components from Name inputs
Only the first instance of matching text is returned from each input file. This is the most efficient way to retrieve a list of files that have contents matching the regular expression.
Maximum degree of parallelism for directory tasks
Maximum file size in bytes to include in results. 0 means unlimited.
Maximum recursion depth for directory traversal. 0 means unlimited.
Maximum recursion depth for continuing searching upwards the tree for relative searches, while no items are found. 0 means disabled.
Minimum file size in bytes to include in results. 0 means no minimum.
Only include files modified after this date/time (UTC).
Only include files modified before this date/time (UTC).
Disables highlighting of matching strings in output.
Forces unattended mode and will not generate links
Do not recurse into subdirectories
The NotMatch parameter finds text that doesn't match the specified pattern.
Output matched items as objects
Indicates that the cmdlet returns a simple response instead of a MatchInfo object. The returned value is $true if the pattern is found or $null if the pattern is not found.
Causes the cmdlet to output only the matching strings, rather than MatchInfo objects. This is the results in behavior that's the most similar to the Unix grep or Windows findstr.exe commands.
Optional: search specific directories
When set, performs content search within alternate data streams (ADS). When not set, outputs ADS file info without searching their content.
Optional: search specific drives

Indicates that the cmdlet uses a simple match rather than a regular expression match. In a simple match, Select-String searches the input for the text in the Pattern parameter. It doesn't interpret the value of the Pattern parameter as a regular expression statement.
Optional: cancellation timeout in seconds

command:parameter.aliases: like, Path, LiteralPath, Query, SearchMask, Include
command:parameter.maml:name: Name

maml:description.maml:para: File name or pattern to search for. Default is '*'
Regular expression pattern to search within content
Base path for resolving relative paths in output
Search across all available drives
Indicates that the cmdlet searches for more than one match in each line of text. Without this parameter, Select-String finds only the first match in each line of text.
File attributes to skip (e.g., System, Hidden or None).
Gets or sets the case-sensitivity for files and directories
Indicates that the cmdlet matches are case-sensitive. By default, matches aren't case-sensitive.
The Category parameter.
Captures the specified number of lines before and after the line that matches the pattern.

Specifies a culture name to match the specified pattern. The Culture parameter must be used with the SimpleMatch parameter. The default behavior uses the culture of the current PowerShell runspace (session).
Search for directories only
Optional: search specific drives
Specifies the type of encoding for the target file. Supports Select-String compatible values and extended .NET encodings.
Exclude files or directories matching these wildcard patterns (e.g., *.tmp, *\bin\*).
Include both files and directories
Follow symlinks and junctions during directory traversal
Include alternate data streams in search results
Include non-text files when searching file contents
Include optical disk drives
File name or pattern to search for. Default is '*'
Enforces searching only within Root directories by stripping directory components from Name inputs
Only the first instance of matching text is returned from each input file. This is the most efficient way to retrieve a list of files that have contents matching the regular expression.
Maximum degree of parallelism for directory tasks
Maximum file size in bytes to include in results. 0 means unlimited.
Maximum recursion depth for directory traversal. 0 means unlimited.
Maximum recursion depth for continuing searching upwards the tree for relative searches, while no items are found. 0 means disabled.
Minimum file size in bytes to include in results. 0 means no minimum.
Only include files modified after this date/time (UTC).
Only include files modified before this date/time (UTC).
Disables highlighting of matching strings in output.
Forces unattended mode and will not generate links
Do not recurse into subdirectories
The NotMatch parameter finds text that doesn't match the specified pattern.
Output matched items as objects
Indicates that the cmdlet returns a simple response instead of a MatchInfo object. The returned value is $true if the pattern is found or $null if the pattern is not found.
Causes the cmdlet to output only the matching strings, rather than MatchInfo objects. This is the results in behavior that's the most similar to the Unix grep or Windows findstr.exe commands.
Optional: search specific directories
When set, performs content search within alternate data streams (ADS). When not set, outputs ADS file info without searching their content.
Optional: search specific drives

Indicates that the cmdlet uses a simple match rather than a regular expression match. In a simple match, Select-String searches the input for the text in the Pattern parameter. It doesn't interpret the value of the Pattern parameter as a regular expression statement.
Optional: cancellation timeout in seconds
Accepts System.Object objects via pipeline.

command:example.maml:title: Find files containing a specific word

dev:remarks.maml:para: Search for all files in the current directory and subdirectories that contain the word "translation".

command:example.maml:title: Find JavaScript files with a version string

dev:remarks.maml:para: Search for JavaScript files containing a version string in the format "Version == `x.y.z`".
Find all directories in the current directory and its subdirectories.

command:example.maml:title: Find XML files and pass objects
command:example.dev:code:
Find-Item ".\*.xml" -PassThru | % FullName
# Short form: l *.xml -pt | % FullName

dev:remarks.maml:para: Search for all .xml files and pass the results as objects through the pipeline.
Search for all files and include their alternate data streams in the results.
Search for all PDF files across all available drives.
Search for log files with a 5-minute timeout and limited parallelism.

command:example.maml:title: Pipeline input
command:example.dev:code:
Get-ChildItem -Path "C:\Logs" | Find-Item -Content "error"
# Short form: ls C:\Logs | l -matchcontent "error"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Pass file paths from Get-ChildItem to search for files containing "error".
Search for text files but limit recursion to 2 directory levels.
Find files larger than 1MB but smaller than 10MB.
Find files modified after January 1, 2025.
Search for all files but exclude temporary files and bin directories.

command:example.maml:title: Search specific drives
command:example.dev:code:
Find-Item "*.docx" -SearchDrives "C:\", "D:\"
# Short form: l *.docx -drives C:\, D:\

dev:remarks.maml:para: Search for .docx files on C: and D: drives only.
Search for files containing "Error" (case-sensitive) in their content.
Search for files with alternate data streams containing "secret".
Search for all occurrences of "function" in each line, not just the first match.
Display 2 lines before and 3 lines after each match for better understanding.
Return just the matching text strings instead of full match objects.
Return true/false instead of match details to check if pattern exists.

command:example.maml:title: Find first match only per file

dev:remarks.maml:para: Stop at the first match in each file for efficient file listing.
Search for exact text without regex interpretation using SimpleMatch.
Use NotMatch to find files that don't contain the specified pattern.

command:example.maml:title: Specify file encoding
command:example.dev:code:
Find-Item "*.txt" -Content "café" -Encoding UTF8
# Short form: l *.txt -mc "café" -Encoding UTF8

dev:remarks.maml:para: Search files with specific encoding for accurate text processing.
Use culture-specific matching with SimpleMatch for international text.

command:example.maml:title: Complex content search with file filters

dev:remarks.maml:para: Combine file size, date, and content filters for precise searches.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the configured default language for image metadata operations.

* This cmdlet retrieves the default language used by the GenXdev.AI module for image metadata operations. * It checks Global variables first (unless SkipSession is specified), then falls back to persistent preferences, and finally uses system defaults.
The default language for image metadata operations
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Database path for preference data files
Use alternative settings stored in session for AI preferences like Language, Image collections, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for AI preferences like Language, Image collections, etc
The default language for image metadata operations
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Database path for preference data files
Use alternative settings stored in session for AI preferences like Language, Image collections, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for AI preferences like Language, Image collections, etc
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates the apparent size of an object at arm's length.
Computes the apparent size using small angle approximation.
The distance to the object in meters
The actual size of the object in meters
The arm length in meters (default: 0.7)
The unit for the output size
The distance to the object in meters
The actual size of the object in meters
The arm length in meters (default: 0.7)
The unit for the output size
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates the apparent size in mm of an object at arm's length.

Computes the size an object would appear to be if viewed at average adult arm's length (approximately 0.7 meters), given its actual size and distance. The calculation uses the small angle approximation for angular size.
The distance to the object in meters.
The actual size of the object in meters.
The arm's length distance in meters. Default value is 0.7.
The distance to the object in meters.
The actual size of the object in meters.
The arm's length distance in meters. Default value is 0.7.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Retrieves the names of available audio devices for microphone or desktop audio capture.
Enumerates audio devices that can be used with other cmdlets that accept an AudioDevice parameter. It supports both microphone devices and desktop audio capture devices.
Returns detailed device objects instead of just names
Whether to list desktop audio capture devices instead of microphone devices
Returns detailed device objects instead of just names
Whether to list desktop audio capture devices instead of microphone devices
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Returns a dictionary of all installed BCP 47 language tags and their display names.

* Builds a dictionary mapping BCP 47 culture codes (e.g. en-US, nl-NL, de-DE) to themselves and their English display names to the codes. * Uses the .NET CultureInfo API to enumerate all specific cultures installed on the system, making this the authoritative source of truth for PowerShell help language folder names and translation target languages. * Dictionary keys include: - Full BCP 47 tags (e.g. "en-US", "nl-NL", "zh-Hans-CN") - English display names (e.g. "English (United States)", "Dutch (Netherlands)") - Simple language names mapped to their most common region (e.g. "English" -> "en-US", "Dutch" -> "nl-NL")
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates buoyant force.
Uses F = ρ V g.
Displaced volume in m³
Fluid density in kg/m³
Gravity in m/s² (default: 9.81)
Output unit for force
Displaced volume in m³
Fluid density in kg/m³
Gravity in m/s² (default: 9.81)
Output unit for force
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: {{ Add example description here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Calculates centripetal acceleration.
Uses a = v² / r.
Velocity in m/s
Radius in meters
Output unit for acceleration
Velocity in m/s
Radius in meters
Output unit for acceleration
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Retrieves all processes that are descendants of the current PowerShell process.

* Examines all running processes and identifies those that have the current PowerShell process as an ancestor in their parent process chain. * This includes both direct child processes and their descendants (grandchildren, etc.).
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: Get child processes with verbose output showing detailed process detection

dev:remarks.maml:para: information.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets files from the Windows clipboard that were set for file operations like copy/paste.

* This function retrieves file paths from the Windows clipboard that were previously set for file operations. * It handles both STA and MTA threading modes automatically, ensuring compatibility across different PowerShell execution contexts. * The function validates file existence and returns only existing files/directories as objects similar to Get-ChildItem or Get-Item output.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates and returns the total number of logical CPU cores in the system.

Queries the system hardware through Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) to determine the total number of logical CPU cores. The function accounts for hyperthreading by multiplying the physical core count by 2.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Retrieves the process object of the window that currently has keyboard focus.

* This cmdlet uses Windows API calls through P/Invoke to identify and return the Process object associated with the currently focused window. * It leverages the User32.dll functions GetForegroundWindow and GetWindowThreadProcessId to determine which window has focus and obtain its associated process ID.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: Retrieves the process object for the window that currently has keyboard

dev:remarks.maml:para: focus.

maml:description.maml:para: Returns the configured default web browser for the current user.

* Retrieves information about the system's default web browser by querying the Windows Registry. * Returns a hashtable containing the browser's name, description, icon path, and executable path. * The function checks both user preferences and system-wide browser registrations to determine the default browser.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Gets the default web language key based on the system's current language settings.
* Retrieves the current system language and culture settings and maps them to the corresponding web language dictionary key used by translation services.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Retrieves the Windows display scaling factor (DPI setting) for a specified monitor.

* Gets the current Windows display scaling factor configured for a monitor in the system. * The scaling factor is expressed as a percentage where 100 represents standard scaling (96 DPI). Common values are 100, 125, 150, and 200. * If no monitor is specified, returns the scaling factor for the primary display.
The monitor index to check (0 = primary monitor)
The monitor index to check (0 = primary monitor)
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: Returns the scaling factor percentage for the second monitor using positional

dev:remarks.maml:para: parameter.

maml:description.maml:para: Calculates Doppler shifted frequency.
Uses f' = f * (v + vo) / (v - vs), speeds positive towards each other.
Original frequency in Hz
Source speed in m/s (positive towards observer)
Observer speed in m/s (positive towards source)
The medium
Speed of sound in m/s (default: 343)
Output unit for frequency
Original frequency in Hz
Source speed in m/s (positive towards observer)
Observer speed in m/s (positive towards source)
The medium
Speed of sound in m/s (default: 343)
Output unit for frequency
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates drag force.
Uses F = 1/2 C ρ A v².
Velocity in m/s
Fluid density in kg/m³
Cross-sectional area in m²
Drag coefficient
Output unit for force
Velocity in m/s
Fluid density in kg/m³
Cross-sectional area in m²
Drag coefficient
Output unit for force
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates escape velocity.
Uses v = sqrt(2 G M / r).
Mass of body in kg
Radius in meters
Output unit for velocity
Mass of body in kg
Radius in meters
Output unit for velocity
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Gets the handle of the currently active foreground window.

* This cmdlet retrieves the window handle (HWND) of the window that is currently in the foreground and has keyboard focus. * It uses Windows API functions through P/Invoke to interact with the User32.dll library.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates the distance fallen during free fall for a given time duration.
Calculates the distance fallen during free fall using a numerical method that accounts for air resistance and terminal velocity.
The time duration of the fall in seconds
The terminal velocity in meters per second (default: 53)
The acceleration due to gravity in m/s² (default: 9.81)
The unit for the output distance
The time duration of the fall in seconds
The terminal velocity in meters per second (default: 53)
The acceleration due to gravity in m/s² (default: 9.81)
The unit for the output distance
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates the height fallen during free fall for a given time duration.
Calculates the distance fallen during free fall using a numerical method that accounts for air resistance and terminal velocity.
The time duration of the fall in seconds
The terminal velocity in meters per second (default: 53 m/s for human)
The unit for the output height
The time duration of the fall in seconds
The terminal velocity in meters per second (default: 53 m/s for human)
The unit for the output height
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates the time required for an object to fall a given height during free fall.

Calculates the time duration required for an object to fall a specified height using a numerical method that accounts for air resistance and terminal velocity. The calculation uses small time steps to accurately model the physics of falling objects with realistic terminal velocity constraints.
The height to fall in meters
The terminal velocity in meters per second (default: 53 m/s for human)
The unit for the output time
The height to fall in meters
The terminal velocity in meters per second (default: 53 m/s for human)
The unit for the output time
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Retrieves a preference value from the GenXdev preferences store.

* Implements a two-tier preference retrieval system. * First checks the local store for a preference value. * If not found, falls back to the default store. * If still not found, returns the provided default value.
The name of the preference to retrieve
The default value if preference is not found
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Database path for preference data files
Use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
The name of the preference to retrieve
The default value if preference is not found
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Database path for preference data files
Use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Accepts System.String objects via pipeline.
Gets all preference names from session storage and database stores.

* Retrieves a unique list of preference names by combining keys from session storage (global variables) and both the local and default preference stores. * Respects session management parameters to control which sources are queried.
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Database path for preference data files
Use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Database path for preference data files
Use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: Returns a sorted array of unique preference names from session storage and

dev:remarks.maml:para: both stores using the specified database path.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the configured database path for preference data files used in GenXdev.Data operations.

* Retrieves the global database path used by the GenXdev.Data module for various preference storage and data operations. * Checks Global variables first (unless SkipSession is specified), then falls back to persistent preferences, and finally uses system defaults.
Optional database path override
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Optional database path override
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Get the list of changed files in a Git repository.

This function retrieves the list of files that have been changed in the current Git repository. It can be used to identify which files have been modified, added, or deleted. By default, returns relative paths with .\ prefix. Use -PassThru to get FileInfo objects.
Returns FileInfo objects (like Get-ChildItem) instead of path strings.
Returns FileInfo objects (like Get-ChildItem) instead of path strings.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Returns objects.
Returns objects.
Determines if a CUDA-capable GPU with sufficient memory is present.

Checks the system for CUDA-compatible GPUs with at least 4GB of video RAM. It uses Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) to query installed video controllers and verify their memory capacity. This check is essential for AI workloads that require significant GPU memory.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Extracts geolocation data from an image file.

* Reads EXIF metadata from an image file to extract its latitude and longitude coordinates. * Supports images that contain GPS metadata in their EXIF data. * Uses the System.Drawing.Image class to load the image and parse the GPS coordinates from property items.
Path to the image file to analyze
Path to the image file to analyze
Accepts System.String objects via pipeline.
Calculates impact velocity from height.
Uses v = sqrt(2 g h) ignoring air resistance.
Height in meters
Gravity in m/s². Default 9.81.
Output unit for velocity. Default 'm/s'.
Height in meters
Gravity in m/s². Default 9.81.
Output unit for velocity. Default 'm/s'.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Returns true if the text-to-speech engine is speaking.
Checks the state of both the default and customized speech synthesizers to determine if either is currently speaking. Returns true if speech is in progress, false otherwise.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Retrieves the names of available key-value stores.

* Scans the key-value store directory for JSON files and extracts unique store names based on the specified synchronization key pattern. * Ensures the store directory exists and synchronizes non-local stores if necessary.
Key to identify synchronization scope, defaults to all
Database path for key-value store data files
Key to identify synchronization scope, defaults to all
Database path for key-value store data files
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Gets the file path for a key-value store.
* Constructs the file path for a key-value store based on the synchronization key, store name, and base directory.
The synchronization key used to identify the store
The name of the key-value store
The base directory path for store files
The synchronization key used to identify the store
The name of the key-value store
The base directory path for store files
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates kinetic energy.
Uses KE = 1/2 m v².
Mass in kg
Velocity in m/s
Output unit for energy
Mass in kg
Velocity in m/s
Output unit for energy
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates time for light to travel a distance.
Uses t = d / c, with c depending on medium.
Distance in meters
The medium through which light travels
Speed of light in m/s (default: 299792458)
Output unit for time
Distance in meters
The medium through which light travels
Speed of light in m/s (default: 299792458)
Output unit for time
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates magnification for a lens.
Uses m = - (image distance / object distance).
Object distance in meters
Image distance in meters
Object distance in meters
Image distance in meters
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates linear momentum.
Uses p = m v.
Mass in kg
Velocity in m/s
Output unit for momentum
Mass in kg
Velocity in m/s
Output unit for momentum
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: {{ Add example description here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the total number of display monitors connected to the system.

* Uses the Windows Presentation Foundation (WPF) Screen helper class to accurately determine the number of physical display monitors currently connected to the system. * This includes both active and detected but disabled monitors.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Gets the path to the Windows Defender MpCmdRun.exe executable.

* Locates and returns the full path to the Windows Defender command-line utility (MpCmdRun.exe). * The cmdlet checks the standard installation location in Program Files and provides appropriate error handling if the file is not found.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Returns a random affirmation text from the affirmations.dev API.
* Retrieves a random affirmation from the affirmations.dev API and optionally speaks it using text-to-speech.
Use text-to-speech to speak the affirmation
Use text-to-speech to speak the affirmation
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates and returns the total number of logical CPU cores in the system.

Queries the system hardware through Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) to determine the total number of logical CPU cores. The cmdlet accounts for hyperthreading by multiplying the physical core count by 2. This information is useful for optimizing parallel processing tasks and understanding system capabilities.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: R
command:example.dev:code: $cores = Get-NumberOfCpuCores Write-Host "System has $cores logical CPU cores available"

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves processes that have open file handles to specified files.

* Uses the Sysinternals handle.exe tool to identify processes that currently have open handles to one or more specified files. * Useful for determining which processes are preventing file operations like deletion or modification. * Requires handle.exe from the Sysinternals suite to be installed and available in the system path.
The path to the file(s) to check for open handles
The path to the file(s) to check for open handles
Accepts System.String[] objects via pipeline.
Calculates orbital velocity.
Uses v = sqrt(G M / r).
Orbital radius in meters
Central mass in kg
Output unit for velocity
Orbital radius in meters
Central mass in kg
Output unit for velocity
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Gets the Playwright browser profile directory for persistent sessions.

* Creates and manages browser profile directories for Playwright automated testing. * Profiles are stored in LocalAppData under GenXdev.Powershell\Playwright.profiles. * Each BrowserType value gets its own subdirectory, keeping real-browser profiles (ChromeNormal, EdgeNormal) separate from bundled Playwright profiles (ChromiumPlaywright, FirefoxPlaywright, WebKitPlaywright).
The browser type: ChromeNormal, EdgeNormal, ChromiumNormal, ChromiumPlaywright, FirefoxPlaywright, or WebKitPlaywright
The browser type: ChromeNormal, EdgeNormal, ChromiumNormal, ChromiumPlaywright, FirefoxPlaywright, or WebKitPlaywright
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates gravitational potential energy.
Uses PE = m g h.
Mass in kg
Height in meters
Gravity in m/s² (default: 9.81)
Output unit for energy
Mass in kg
Height in meters
Gravity in m/s² (default: 9.81)
Output unit for energy
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Returns a window helper object for the PowerShell terminal's main window.
* Retrieves a WindowObj helper object that represents the main window of the current PowerShell host process. * This allows manipulation and interaction with the terminal window itself.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Returns the process object for the window hosting the PowerShell terminal.

* Traverses up the process tree starting from the current PowerShell process to locate the parent window responsible for hosting the terminal. * If the immediate parent process doesn't have a main window handle, it searches for similar processes that do have main windows. * Useful for identifying the actual terminal window process (like Windows Terminal, ConHost, etc.) that contains the PowerShell session.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: Returns the process hosting the current PowerShell session and displays its

dev:remarks.maml:para: name.

maml:description.maml:para: Calculates the range of a projectile.
Uses the formula R = (v² sin(2θ)) / g for ideal projectile motion.
Initial speed in m/s
Launch angle in degrees
Gravity in m/s² (default: 9.81)
Output unit for range
Initial speed in m/s
Launch angle in degrees
Gravity in m/s² (default: 9.81)
Output unit for range
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Generates a detailed report of refactoring operations and their status.

Analyzes and reports on the progress of refactoring operations by examining their current state, completion status, and affected functions. Provides output in either structured hashtable format or human-readable aligned text columns. The report includes refactor name, prompt key, priority, status, function count and completion percentage.
The name of the refactor, accepts wildcards
Output report in text format instead of Hashtable
If set, clear the session cache before running.
Specifies the path to the preferences database file.
If set, only use the session cache for refactor data.
If set, skip loading session cache.
The name of the refactor, accepts wildcards
Output report in text format instead of Hashtable
If set, clear the session cache before running.
Specifies the path to the preferences database file.
If set, only use the session cache for refactor data.
If set, skip loading session cache.
Accepts System.String[] objects via pipeline.
Returns objects.
Returns objects.
Calculates refraction angle using Snell's law.
Uses θ2 = arcsin( (n1 / n2) sin θ1 ).
Incident angle in degrees
Refractive index of first medium
Refractive index of second medium
Output unit for angle
Incident angle in degrees
Refractive index of first medium
Refractive index of second medium
Output unit for angle
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates resonant frequency for a closed pipe.
Uses f = v / (4 L) for fundamental frequency.
Length in meters
The medium
Wave speed in m/s
Output unit for frequency
Length in meters
The medium
Wave speed in m/s
Output unit for frequency
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates the distance sound travels in a given time.
Uses the formula distance = speed * time, with default speed of sound in air.
The time in seconds
The medium through which sound travels
Speed of sound in m/s (default: 343)
The unit for the output distance
The time in seconds
The medium through which sound travels
Speed of sound in m/s (default: 343)
The unit for the output distance
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Converts audio files to text using OpenAI's Whisper speech recognition model.

Processes audio files and converts speech to text using the Whisper.NET library, which implements OpenAI's Whisper automatic speech recognition (ASR) system. It supports multiple languages, translation capabilities, and various transcription quality settings.
Audio file path, FileInfo object, or any audio format supported by Whisper.
Size of the audio context
Sets the output language
Whether to NOT suppress blank lines
Entropy threshold
Sets the input language to detect, defaults to 'en'
Length penalty
Log probability threshold
Maximum duration of the audio
Start timestamps at this moment
Maximum number of last text tokens
Maximum segment length
Maximum number of tokens per segment
Path to the model file directory
Whisper model type to use, defaults to LargeV3Turbo
Don't use context
No speech threshold
Offset for the audio
Returns objects instead of strings
Whether to print special tokens
Prompt to use for the model
Whether to use single segment only
Whether to split on word boundaries
Regex to suppress tokens from the output
Temperature for speech detection
Temperature increment
Sum threshold for token timestamps, defaults to 0.5
Use beam search sampling strategy
Whether to show progress
Whether to include token timestamps
Whether to translate the output
Audio file path, FileInfo object, or any audio format supported by Whisper.
Size of the audio context
Sets the output language
Whether to NOT suppress blank lines
Entropy threshold
Sets the input language to detect, defaults to 'en'
Length penalty
Log probability threshold
Maximum duration of the audio
Start timestamps at this moment
Maximum number of last text tokens
Maximum segment length
Maximum number of tokens per segment
Path to the model file directory
Whisper model type to use, defaults to LargeV3Turbo
Don't use context
No speech threshold
Offset for the audio
Returns objects instead of strings
Whether to print special tokens
Prompt to use for the model
Whether to use single segment only
Whether to split on word boundaries
Regex to suppress tokens from the output
Temperature for speech detection
Temperature increment
Sum threshold for token timestamps, defaults to 0.5
Use beam search sampling strategy
Whether to show progress
Whether to include token timestamps
Whether to translate the output
Accepts System.Object objects via pipeline.
Returns objects.
Returns objects.
Retrieves all key names for a given key-value store.

* Queries the KeyValueStore JSON file to retrieve all active (non-deleted) keys for a specified store. * Can optionally filter by synchronization scope. * Automatically initializes the directory structure if not found and handles synchronization for non-local stores.
Name of the store whose keys should be retrieved
Key to identify synchronization scope, defaults to all
Database path for key-value store data files
Name of the store whose keys should be retrieved
Key to identify synchronization scope, defaults to all
Database path for key-value store data files
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates terminal velocity.
Uses v = sqrt(2 m g / (ρ A C)).
Mass in kg
Gravity in m/s² (default: 9.81)
Fluid density in kg/m³
Cross-sectional area in m²
Drag coefficient (default: 0.5)
Output unit for velocity
Mass in kg
Gravity in m/s² (default: 9.81)
Fluid density in kg/m³
Cross-sectional area in m²
Drag coefficient (default: 0.5)
Output unit for velocity
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates the time of flight for a projectile.
Uses T = (2 v sinθ) / g for ideal motion.
Initial velocity in m/s
Launch angle in degrees
Gravity in m/s² (default: 9.81)
Output unit for time
Initial velocity in m/s
Launch angle in degrees
Gravity in m/s² (default: 9.81)
Output unit for time
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Retrieves a value from a JSON-based key-value store.

* Retrieves a value for a specified key from a JSON file-based key-value store. * Supports optional default values and synchronization across different scopes. * Provides automatic directory initialization and synchronization.
Name of the store to retrieve the key from
Key to retrieve from the specified store
A optional default value
Key to identify synchronization scope
Database path for key-value store data files
Name of the store to retrieve the key from
Key to retrieve from the specified store
A optional default value
Key to identify synchronization scope
Database path for key-value store data files
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: Retrieves the "Theme" value from "AppSettings", returning "Dark" if not

dev:remarks.maml:para: found.

maml:description.maml:para: Calculates the cosine similarity between two vectors.

Takes two numerical vectors (arrays) as input and computes their cosine similarity. The result indicates how closely related the vectors are, with 0 meaning completely dissimilar and 1 meaning identical.
First vector array of numbers
Second vector array of numbers
First vector array of numbers
Second vector array of numbers
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Calculates wave speed.
Uses v = f λ.
Frequency in Hz
Wavelength in meters
Output unit for speed
Frequency in Hz
Wavelength in meters
Output unit for speed
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Returns a collection of installed modern web browsers.

* Discovers and returns details about modern web browsers installed on the system. * Retrieves information including name, description, icon path, executable path and default browser status by querying the Windows registry. * Only returns browsers that have the required capabilities registered in Windows.
Selects Google Chrome browser instances
Selects default chromium-based browser
Selects Microsoft Edge browser instances
Selects Firefox browser instances
Selects Google Chrome browser instances
Selects default chromium-based browser
Selects Microsoft Edge browser instances
Selects Firefox browser instances
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Returns a reversed dictionary for all languages supported by Google Search.

* Returns a dictionary mapping language names to their corresponding language codes for all languages supported by Google Search. * Useful for language code lookups in web queries and translation tasks.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Gets window information for specified processes or window handles.

* Retrieves window information using process name, ID, or window handle. * Returns WindowObj objects containing details about the main windows of matching processes. * Supports wildcards when searching by process name.
Name of the process to get window information for
ID of the process to get window information for
Window handle to get information for
Name of the process to get window information for
ID of the process to get window information for
Window handle to get information for
Accepts System.Int32 objects via pipeline.
Accepts System.Int64 objects via pipeline.
Accepts System.String objects via pipeline.
Initializes KeyValueStore directory structure for local and OneDrive storage.

Creates directory structure for JSON-based key-value stores in two locations: local machine for immediate access and OneDrive folder for cloud synchronization. The cmdlet ensures both directories exist and are properly configured.
Database path for key-value store data files
Database path for key-value store data files
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Initializes and configures system search paths for package management.

* Builds a comprehensive list of search paths by combining default system locations, chocolatey paths, development tool paths, and custom package paths. * Updates the system's PATH environment variable with these consolidated paths.
The workspace folder path to use for node modules and PowerShell paths.
The workspace folder path to use for node modules and PowerShell paths.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Moves files and directories while preserving filesystem links and references.

* Uses the Windows MoveFileEx API to move files and directories with link tracking enabled, ensuring filesystem references are maintained. * If the source path is under Git control, it attempts to use git mv to track the rename in Git. * Falls back to MoveFileEx if Git is not available or git mv fails.
Source path of file/directory to move
Destination path to move to
Overwrite destination if it exists
Source path of file/directory to move
Destination path to move to
Overwrite destination if it exists
Accepts System.String objects via pipeline.
Moves files and directories to the Windows Recycle Bin safely.

* Safely moves files or directories to the recycle bin using the Windows Shell API, even if they are currently in use. * The function uses the Shell.Application COM object to perform the operation, ensuring proper recycling behavior and undo capability.
Specify the path(s) to move to the recycle bin
Specify the path(s) to move to the recycle bin
Accepts System.String[] objects via pipeline.
Creates and pushes a new git commit with all changes.
Stages all changes in the current git repository, creates a commit with the specified title, and pushes the changes to the remote origin. Automatically sets up upstream tracking if needed.
The commit message title to use
The commit message title to use
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Converts real-time audio input to text using Whisper AI model.

This cmdlet captures audio from microphone or desktop and transcribes it to text in real-time using the Whisper AI model. It supports various audio sources, silence detection, and multiple configuration options for speech recognition.
Size of the audio context
Use both desktop and recording device
Sets the output language
Whether to NOT suppress blank lines
Entropy threshold
Whether to ignore silence (will mess up timestamps)
Sets the input language to detect, defaults to 'en'
Length penalty
Log probability threshold
Maximum duration of the audio
Maximum duration of silence before automatically stopping recording
Start timestamps at this moment
Maximum number of last text tokens
Maximum segment length
Maximum number of tokens per segment
Path to the model file
Whisper model type to use, defaults to Small
Don't use context
No speech threshold
Offset for the audio
Returns objects instead of strings
Whether to print special tokens
Prompt to use for the model
Silence detect threshold (0..32767 defaults to 30)
Whether to use single segment only
Whether to split on word boundaries
Regex to suppress tokens from the output
Temperature for speech detection
Temperature increment
Sum threshold for token timestamps, defaults to 0.5
Whether to use both desktop audio capture and recording device simultaneously
Whether to use desktop audio capture instead of microphone
Use beam search sampling strategy
Whether to show progress
Whether to include token timestamps
Whether to translate the output
Size of the audio context
Use both desktop and recording device
Sets the output language
Whether to NOT suppress blank lines
Entropy threshold
Whether to ignore silence (will mess up timestamps)
Sets the input language to detect, defaults to 'en'
Length penalty
Log probability threshold
Maximum duration of the audio
Maximum duration of silence before automatically stopping recording
Start timestamps at this moment
Maximum number of last text tokens
Maximum segment length
Maximum number of tokens per segment
Path to the model file
Whisper model type to use, defaults to Small
Don't use context
No speech threshold
Offset for the audio
Returns objects instead of strings
Whether to print special tokens
Prompt to use for the model
Silence detect threshold (0..32767 defaults to 30)
Whether to use single segment only
Whether to split on word boundaries
Regex to suppress tokens from the output
Temperature for speech detection
Temperature increment
Sum threshold for token timestamps, defaults to 0.5
Whether to use both desktop audio capture and recording device simultaneously
Whether to use desktop audio capture instead of microphone
Use beam search sampling strategy
Whether to show progress
Whether to include token timestamps
Whether to translate the output
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Removes a preference value from the GenXdev preferences store.

* Removes a preference value from the local store and optionally from the default store. * Provides two parameter sets - one for local removal only and another for removing from both local and default stores. * Ensures proper synchronization when modifying the default store.
The name of the preference to remove
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Database path for preference data files
Switch to also remove the preference from defaults
Use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
The name of the preference to remove
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Database path for preference data files
Switch to also remove the preference from defaults
Use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Accepts System.String objects via pipeline.
Removes comments from JSON content.

* Processes JSON content and removes both single-line and multi-line comments while preserving the JSON structure. * Useful for cleaning up JSON files that contain documentation comments before parsing. * Supports both single-line comments (//) and multi-line comments (/* */).
JSON content to process as string array
JSON content to process as string array
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: R

maml:description.maml:para: Removes a key from a key-value store.

This function removes a specified key from a named key-value store. For local stores, the key is permanently deleted. For synchronized stores, the key is marked as deleted to allow for synchronization.
Name of the store
Key to be deleted
Key to identify synchronization scope
Database path for key-value store data files
Name of the store
Key to be deleted
Key to identify synchronization scope
Database path for key-value store data files
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Removes a key-value store.
This function deletes a specified key-value store. For local stores, it physically removes the file. For synchronized stores, it marks all keys as deleted and triggers synchronization.
Name of the store to delete
Key to identify synchronization scope
Database path for key-value store data files
Name of the store to delete
Key to identify synchronization scope
Database path for key-value store data files
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Marks files or directories for deletion during the next system boot.

* Uses the Windows API to mark files for deletion on next boot. * Handles locked files by first attempting to rename them to temporary names and tracks all moves to maintain file system integrity. * If renaming fails, the -MarkInPlace parameter can be used to mark files in their original location.
Path(s) to files/directories to mark for deletion
Marks files for deletion without renaming
Path(s) to files/directories to mark for deletion
Marks files for deletion without renaming
Accepts System.String[] objects via pipeline.

command:example.maml:title: Marks multiple files for deletion, using MarkInPlace for any that can't be

dev:remarks.maml:para: renamed.

maml:description.maml:para: Captures a screenshot of the specified monitor and saves it to the specified path.

* Takes a screenshot of the desktop from the specified monitor and saves it as an image file. * Supports multiple monitors and automatically detects the correct screen bounds and scaling factors for high-DPI displays.
Monitor index to capture (0=primary, 1+=specific monitor, -1=all monitors)
Full path where the screenshot will be saved
JPEG quality level (1-100), only applies to JPEG format
Monitor index to capture (0=primary, 1+=specific monitor, -1=all monitors)
Full path where the screenshot will be saved
JPEG quality level (1-100), only applies to JPEG format
Accepts System.Int32 objects via pipeline.
Accepts System.String objects via pipeline.
Brings the specified window to the foreground and makes it the active window.
* Makes a window the foreground window using multiple Win32 API methods for maximum reliability. * First attempts using SwitchToThisWindow API, then falls back to SetForegroundWindow if needed.
Window handle to set as foreground window
Window handle to set as foreground window
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Sets a default preference value in the GenXdev preferences store.

* Manages default preferences in the GenXdev preference system. * Handles storing values, removing preferences when values are empty, and ensures changes are synchronized across the system. * Supports null values by removing the preference entirely in such cases.
The name of the preference to set in defaults
The value to store for the preference
Database path for preference data files
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
The name of the preference to set in defaults
The value to store for the preference
Database path for preference data files
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Accepts System.String objects via pipeline.
Sets a preference value in the GenXdev preferences store.

* Manages preferences in the GenXdev local store. * Can set new preferences, update existing ones, or remove them when a null/empty value is provided. * Preferences are stored with synchronization set to "Local".
The name of the preference to set
The value to store for the preference
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Database path for preference data files
Use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
The name of the preference to set
The value to store for the preference
Clear the session setting (Global variable) before retrieving
Database path for preference data files
Use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
Accepts System.String objects via pipeline.
Sets the database path for preferences used in GenXdev.Data operations.

* Configures the global database path used by the GenXdev.Data module for various preference storage and data operations. * Settings are stored in the current session (using Global variables) and can be cleared from the session (using -ClearSession).
A database path where preference data files are located
When specified, clears only the session setting (Global variable) without affecting persistent preferences
When specified, stores the setting only in the current session (Global variable) without persisting to preferences
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
A database path where preference data files are located
When specified, clears only the session setting (Global variable) without affecting persistent preferences
When specified, stores the setting only in the current session (Global variable) without persisting to preferences
Dont use alternative settings stored in session for Data preferences like Language, Database paths, etc
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Changes the current location to the parent directory and lists its contents.
* Navigates up one directory level in the file system hierarchy. * Displays the contents of the new current directory. * Provides a convenient '..' alias for quick directory navigation.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Navigates up two directory levels in the file system hierarchy.
* Changes the current working directory to the grandparent directory (two levels up). * Displays the contents of the resulting directory.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Navigates up three directory levels in the file system hierarchy.
* Changes the current working directory by moving up three parent directories in the filesystem hierarchy. * Displays the contents of the resulting directory.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Navigates up four directory levels in the filesystem hierarchy.
* Moves up four directory levels from the current location in the filesystem. * After navigation, displays the contents of the resulting directory.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Navigates up five directory levels in the file system hierarchy.

* Changes the current working directory by moving up five parent directory levels. * Displays the contents of the resulting directory. * Provides a convenient shorthand alias '......' for quick navigation.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Turns off power to all connected monitors.

Uses Windows API calls to send a power-off signal to all connected monitors. This is equivalent to pressing the physical power button on your monitors. The monitors will enter power saving mode but can be awakened by moving the mouse or pressing a key.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Turns the monitor power on.
Uses the Windows API through GenXdev.Helpers.WindowObj to wake up the monitor from sleep/power off state. This is useful for automation scripts that need to ensure the monitor is powered on.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Manages key-value pairs in a JSON file-based store.

Provides persistent storage for key-value pairs using JSON files. Handles both insertion of new entries and updates to existing ones. Supports optional synchronization for non-local stores. This function implements an upsert operation that either inserts new key-value pairs or updates existing ones based on the combination of synchronization key, store name, and key name.
Store name for the key-value pair
Name of the key to set or update
Value to be stored
Key to identify synchronization scope
Database path for key-value store data files
Store name for the key-value pair
Name of the key to set or update
Value to be stored
Key to identify synchronization scope
Database path for key-value store data files
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Sets a random wallpaper from a specified directory.
* Selects a random image file from the specified directory and sets it as the Windows desktop wallpaper. * Supports JPG/JPEG image formats and configures the wallpaper to "fit" the screen by default.
Path to the directory containing the wallpaper images
Search across all available drives
Do not recurse into subdirectories
Path to the directory containing the wallpaper images
Search across all available drives
Do not recurse into subdirectories
Accepts System.Object objects via pipeline.
Starts a process with a specified priority level.

* Launches an executable with a customizable priority level and provides options for waiting and process handling. * Wraps Start-Process with additional functionality to control process priority and execution behavior.
Path to the executable to run
Arguments to pass to the executable
Process priority level
Don't wait for process completion
Return the process object
Path to the executable to run
Arguments to pass to the executable
Process priority level
Don't wait for process completion
Return the process object
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Synchronizes local and OneDrive key-value store JSON files.

Performs two-way synchronization between local and OneDrive shadow JSON files using a last-modified timestamp strategy. Records are merged based on their last modification time, with newer versions taking precedence.
Key to identify synchronization scope
Database path for key-value store data files
Key to identify synchronization scope
Database path for key-value store data files
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Tests whether the CPU supports AVX and AVX2 instruction sets.

* Checks if the processor supports AVX and AVX2 instruction sets using Windows API (kernel32.dll IsProcessorFeaturePresent). * Returns an object with AVX and AVX2 boolean properties. * Useful for determining hardware compatibility for AI/ML workloads.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Scans files or directories for malware using Windows Defender.

* Performs a targeted scan of specified files or directories using Windows Defender's command-line interface (MpCmdRun.exe). * Can scan in detection-only mode or with automatic threat remediation enabled. * Returns true if no threats are detected, false if threats are found or the scan fails.
The path to the file or directory to be scanned
Instructs Windows Defender to take action on threats
The path to the file or directory to be scanned
Instructs Windows Defender to take action on threats
Accepts System.String objects via pipeline.
Detects if PowerShell is running in unattended/automated mode.

* Analyzes various indicators to determine if PowerShell is running in an unattended or automated context. * Checks pipeline analysis, environment variables, console redirection, and invocation context. * When CallersInvocation is provided, analyzes the pipeline position and count to detect automated pipeline or script execution.
Caller's invocation info for pipeline and automation detection
Return detailed analysis object instead of simple boolean
Caller's invocation info for pipeline and automation detection
Return detailed analysis object instead of simple boolean
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Returns objects.
Returns objects.

command:example.maml:title: E

maml:description.maml:para: Writes bytes to a file atomically to prevent corruption.

* Atomic write: uses a temp-file + rename strategy to ensure the target file is never left in a corrupted state if the process is interrupted. * Retry logic: retries the write up to MaxRetries times with a delay of RetryDelayMs milliseconds between attempts. * Debounce support: when DebounceMs > 0, rapid consecutive writes to the same file are coalesced — only the last payload is written once the file hasn't been touched for DebounceMs ms. * Directory creation: creates parent directories automatically if they don't exist.
The path to the file to write.
The byte array to write to the file.
If greater than 0, debounce writes to this file. Default is 0 (disabled).
The maximum number of retry attempts for the atomic write operation. Default is 4.
The delay in milliseconds between retry attempts. Default is 200.
The path to the file to write.
The byte array to write to the file.
If greater than 0, debounce writes to this file. Default is 0 (disabled).
The maximum number of retry attempts for the atomic write operation. Default is 4.
The delay in milliseconds between retry attempts. Default is 200.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: Rapidly writes 100 times but only the final payload (100) is persisted

dev:remarks.maml:para: to disk after a 5-second quiet period.

maml:description.maml:para: Writes an object as JSON to a file atomically to prevent corruption.

* Atomic write: uses a temp-file + rename strategy to ensure the target file is never left in a corrupted state if the process is interrupted. * Retry logic: retries the write up to MaxRetries times with a delay of RetryDelayMs milliseconds between attempts. * Object support: accepts any object, not just Hashtable. Serializes via System.Text.Json with fallback to ConvertTo-Json for complex .NET types that can't be serialized natively. * Debounce support: when DebounceMs > 0, rapid consecutive writes to the same file are coalesced — only the last payload is written once the file hasn't been touched for DebounceMs ms. * Directory creation: creates parent directories automatically if they don't exist.
The path to the file to write.
The object to serialize as JSON and write to the file.
Write Compress JSON (no indentation/whitespace).
Write Compress JSON (no indentation/whitespace).
If greater than 0, debounce writes to this file. Default is 0 (disabled).
The maximum depth for JSON serialization. Default is 30.
Provides an alternative serialization option that converts all enumerations to their string representation.
The maximum number of retry attempts for the atomic write operation. Default is 4.
The delay in milliseconds between retry attempts. Default is 200.
The path to the file to write.
The object to serialize as JSON and write to the file.
Write Compress JSON (no indentation/whitespace).
Write Compress JSON (no indentation/whitespace).
If greater than 0, debounce writes to this file. Default is 0 (disabled).
The maximum depth for JSON serialization. Default is 30.
Provides an alternative serialization option that converts all enumerations to their string representation.
The maximum number of retry attempts for the atomic write operation. Default is 4.
The delay in milliseconds between retry attempts. Default is 200.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.
Writes text to a file atomically to prevent corruption.

* Atomic write: uses a temp-file + rename strategy to ensure the target file is never left in a corrupted state if the process is interrupted. * Retry logic: retries the write up to MaxRetries times with a delay of RetryDelayMs milliseconds between attempts. * Encoding support: accepts a System.Text.Encoding parameter to control the output encoding. Defaults to UTF-8. * Debounce support: when DebounceMs > 0, rapid consecutive writes to the same file are coalesced — only the last payload is written once the file hasn't been touched for DebounceMs ms. * Directory creation: creates parent directories automatically if they don't exist.
The path to the file to write.
The text content to write to the file.
If greater than 0, debounce writes to this file. Default is 0 (disabled).
The text encoding to use when writing the file. Default is UTF-8.
The maximum number of retry attempts for the atomic write operation. Default is 4.
The delay in milliseconds between retry attempts. Default is 200.
The path to the file to write.
The text content to write to the file.
If greater than 0, debounce writes to this file. Default is 0 (disabled).
The text encoding to use when writing the file. Default is UTF-8.
The maximum number of retry attempts for the atomic write operation. Default is 4.
The delay in milliseconds between retry attempts. Default is 200.
You can't pipe input to this cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: Pipes 50 lines rapidly; only the final line is persisted after a

dev:remarks.maml:para: 2-second quiet period.

command:example.maml:title: A background job writes timestamps every 100ms but debounce coalesces

dev:remarks.maml:para: them — only one write per second actually hits disk.
